title: ctenopteris pediculata
intro: Ctenopteris pediculata, also known as the club fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce pain and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Ctenopteris pediculata, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to address various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
